chunk_summary: The three-minute OPD audit evaluates Anganwadi supplementary nutrition delivery, focusing on registration, consumption, preparation, and interruptions, to identify and escalate program failures for corrective action.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of Anganwadi services in providing supplementary nutrition under NFSA standards.
Learn how to conduct a three-minute OPD audit to assess nutrition receipt and consumption.
Identify barriers to effective supplementary nutrition delivery and propose corrective actions.
Document and escalate program failures through appropriate governance channels.
key_facts: Supplementary nutrition is a legal entitlement under the National Food Security Act (NFSA), Schedule II.
The audit focuses on four areas: registration, receipt versus consumption, preparation and palatability, and interruptions.
Repeated interruptions in nutrition delivery (e.g., stock-outs, Anganwadi closures) are considered governance failures.
Audit documentation includes anthropometry trends, feeding history, and a summary of nutrition receipt and consumption.
Escalation of issues follows a duty ladder: AWW → supervisor/sector in-charge → CDPO.
Evidence-based supportive care measures are essential
reference_quote: Supplementary nutrition through Anganwadi services is a legal entitlement anchored in the NFSA Schedule II nutrition standards, but state menus and delivery logistics vary.
